behavior_summary: CHEST_EW::look: When a player (or process) examines the chest, the chest emits a short identifying text ('chest ') via Item::bark and the handler yields/suspends (typical U8P_CHEST_EW::look behavior).

CHEST_EW::use: Toggles the chest between open and closed. On use it plays a chest sound, reads the current frame to determine open vs closed, and either requests opening (set open_request_flag) or closes the chest UI (Item::closeGump).

If the game loop is active, the use routine iterates contained tokens/items and invokes their use handlers (inferred via loopscr + calli/process) to update or notify contents when the chest opens/closes.

The use handler suspends (yields) to allow asynchronous/UI operations, then on resume it re-checks frame/state and either opens the chest UI gump (Item::openGump with parameter 7) if the open request still applies, or applies a computed frame change (Item::setFrame) to reflect the new open/closed state.
notable_control_flow: Branch on current Item::getFrame to decide opening vs closing path.
When closing: immediately call Item::closeGump and prepare frame_delta = -1.
When opening: set an open_request_flag and prepare frame_delta = +1, then (after iterating contents and suspending) call Item::openGump only if the frame/state did not change while suspended.
Iterative loop over contained items only runs if isGameRunning() returns true (guarding content processing to live runtime).
Suspension/yield point in the use handler: state is saved (frame saved) before suspend and revalidated after resume to avoid racing with external changes.
open_questions: What exact token/structure format is used by the loopscr iteration (the script iterates contained tokens but the token/address format is not explicit)?
How (if at all) the 'quality' value returned by Item::getQuality affects subsequent behavior or sound selection (quality is read but not otherwise used in the visible logic).
Exact meaning of the numeric parameter '7' passed to Item::openGump (gump id, mode, or layout variant) is inferred but not concretely documented here.

Whether additional external actors/processes can change the chest frame during suspension and what side effects those changes might have beyond skipping openGump (the script detects frame changes but downstream implications are unclear).

main_function.name: U8P_CHEST_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the item's short description response when examined: push the literal 'chest ' and invoke Item::bark so the chest announces itself, then yield/suspend as a typical U8P_CHEST_EW::look handler.
entry_conditions: Event 0: triggered when an actor/player looks at or examines the chest (the item's 'U8P_CHEST_EW::look' event).

function_map.name: U8P_CHEST_EW::look
function_map.role: Respond to examine/U8P_CHEST_EW::look by invoking Item::bark with 'chest ' and yielding; primary player-facing description handler.

function_map.name: U8P_CHEST_EW::use
function_map.role: Handle user 'use' on the chest: toggle open/closed, play SFX, close/open chest gump, iterate and notify contained items, suspend and finalize frame/Gump state after resume.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the short identifying text 'chest ' to the viewer/process when examined.
Read the item's quality/variant at use start; value stored for possible use/branching (observed but not used further in visible logic).
Obtain the current display/frame index to determine whether the chest is open or closed and to compute the frame update.
Play chest-use sound effect immediately when the chest is activated.
Close the chest's UI gump when the chest is being closed.
Guard content-iteration so contained-item processing only happens while the game loop is active.
Invoke the use handler on each contained token/item during iteration to update or notify contents when the chest toggles.
Open the chest's UI gump (parameter 7) after resume if the open request condition still holds and the frame wasn't changed externally.
Apply the computed frame change (frame + frame_delta) to visually reflect the chest's new open/closed state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: CHEST_EW::look: When a player (or process) examines the chest, the chest emits a short identifying text ('chest ') via Item::bark and the handler yields/suspends (typical U8P_CHEST_EW::look behavior).
